notes: Cross-session memory for AV Intelligence Digest Agent v5.0. Read at session start (Task 0). Written back at session end (Task 7). Schema v2.0 adds source_failures, company_tracking, consecutive_no_change_days, conflict_age_days.

ongoing_stories.headline: China nationwide AV permit freeze
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active - Day 38 as of June 6, 2026. No resumption timeline confirmed. Cooling-off restricts newly-added vehicles through at least end of June (CarNewsChina, May 29). Enforcement more moderate than initially signaled. Three joint-ministry (MIIT/MPS/MOT) meetings mandated swarm resilience stress tests. Existing permitted services continue. Bloomberg (June 4) adds employment-protection dimension to regulatory risk.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Outage trigger date disputed: March 31, 2026 (EVWire/CarNewsChina/CNBC, localized Wuhan incident, Score: 3) vs April 27, 2026 (Bloomberg/Automotive World, system-wide multi-city recurrence, Score: 3). Resolution search June 6 confirmed both dates reference different events; no official statement. Working conclusion: April 27 system-wide event most likely triggered April 29 MIIT freeze; March 31 was precursor. LOW PRIORITY.
status_change_triggers: Official MIIT or Ministry of Transport announcement on resumption or extension
Any operator receiving a new AV permit
Any official timeline given or revised
Any additional operator sanctioned or penalized
Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the disputed outage trigger date
Any regulatory document citing a specific confirmed date

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go outage trigger date - March 31 vs April 27 conflict
ongoing_stories.last_status: Unresolved as of June 6, 2026. Resolution search conducted June 6 confirmed both dates reference separate events. No new official Baidu/MIIT statement. Working conclusion: April 27 system-wide multi-city event triggered April 29 MIIT freeze; March 31 was Wuhan-only precursor.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: March 31 cited by EVWire/CarNewsChina/CNBC (Score: 3 - multiple Tier 3 corroborations, specific date). April 27 cited by Bloomberg/Automotive World (Score: 3 - Tier 3, two corroborations, system-wide scope). Resolution search June 6: confirmed both are real events; no resolution from official source. LOW PRIORITY.
status_change_triggers: Official Baidu statement confirming or clarifying the date
Regulatory document from MIIT citing a specific date
Third-party independent investigation finding published by credible source

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo NHTSA recall - 3,791 vehicles, flooding defect
ongoing_stories.last_status: Open - permanent remedy still being developed as of June 6, 2026. NHTSA flagged in writing that no permanent fix received. Interim OTA fix deployed (tighter weather restrictions, updated map data). San Antonio and Atlanta operations remain paused.
status_change_triggers: Permanent fix submitted to or approved by NHTSA
Recall scope expanded or narrowed
NHTSA closes or escalates the investigation
Waymo issues a new interim fix or OTA update
San Antonio or Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo freeway ride suspension - construction zone edge cases
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing Day 16 as of June 6, 2026. Surface streets operational in SF, LA, Phoenix, Miami. All freeway rides suspended since May 21, 2026. Root cause not publicly disclosed. No resumption date given.
status_change_triggers: Official freeway resumption announced by Waymo
Suspension extended to additional cities
Root cause publicly disclosed
Software fix confirmed deployed via OTA
Atlanta operations formally resumed

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go Wuhan service shutdown - ongoing post-outage
ongoing_stories.last_status: Still shut down as of June 6, 2026 (Day 67+). No official Apollo Go or Baidu announcement of resumption. No official MIIT document confirms resumption. Unverified tourist guide suggests possible operational status but Score: 0. HIGH PRIORITY conflict - recommend direct verification via Apollo Go app or Baidu IR.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Registry/Automotive World/CNBC/AlphaPilot: service shut down since April 1, investigation ongoing (Score: 4). China Survival Kit tourist guide (unverified, undated): service operational 24/7, outage was 12-hour disruption (Score: 0). Resolution search June 6: no official resumption found. HIGH PRIORITY.
status_change_triggers: Official Apollo Go or Baidu announcement of Wuhan service resumption
Regulatory approval granted for Wuhan operations to restart
Root cause of outage publicly disclosed by Baidu
Credible Tier 1-3 source confirming service operational status in Wuhan

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo Ojai - US Senate scrutiny of Zeekr/Geely manufacturing partnership
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active as of June 6, 2026. No new Senate action or legislation detected this run. Senator Moreno criticism at Senate Commerce Committee hearing remains last known development. Waymo maintains compliance with 2027 connected-vehicle rules. California Ojai commercial fare approval status unknown.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Senate hearing date unconfirmed in sources reviewed - LOW PRIORITY. No new corroboration found June 6 run.
status_change_triggers: Legislation introduced targeting Chinese-manufactured AV vehicles
California PUC issues or denies Ojai commercial fare approval
USTR or Commerce Dept opens formal review of Waymo-Zeekr arrangement
Senate committee issues formal report or subpoena
Waymo changes or terminates Zeekr manufacturing agreement
